data.title: Sprint SC coverage validation: verify all epic success criteria are covered by children
data.description:
## Context

When sprint begins executing an epic, it currently checks whether stories have implementation tasks but does not validate that the epic's success criteria (SCs) are fully covered by its children. This means an epic can pass through preplanning and implementation planning with gaps — SCs that no child story or task addresses. These gaps are only caught late (during completion verification), wasting sub-agent execution time.

## Success Criteria

- Sprint Phase 1 (Preplanning Gate) includes an SC coverage check that cross-references each epic SC against children's done definitions
- When gaps are detected and children are stories, the sprint routes to /dso:preplanning to decompose the missing SCs into new stories
- When gaps are detected and children are tasks, the sprint routes to /dso:implementation-plan to create tasks covering the missing SCs
- The check runs before batch preparation (Phase 3), not after execution
- A test validates that an epic with 5 SCs and children covering only 3 triggers the gap detection and routing

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Add an SC-to-children coverage mapping step in sprint Phase 1 after the existing children readiness check. For each SC, scan children descriptions/done definitions for keyword overlap or explicit SC references. Report unmapped SCs and route to the appropriate decomposition skill based on children type.
